author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society ruled by Big Brother, exploring themes of surveillance, control, and truth.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: The story of young Scout Finch and her father Atticus, a lawyer defending a Black man falsely accused of rape in the segregated South.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A portrait of the Jazz Age, following the mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby and his tragic obsession with the beautiful Daisy Buchanan.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
description: A lone astronaut must use his scientific wits to solve an extinction-level threat to Earth, aided by an unexpected alien ally.

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
author: Douglas Adams
description: A comedic sci-fi adventure following Arthur Dent, who is rescued by an alien friend just before Earth is demolished to make way for a hyperspace bypass.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: An allegorical novel about a Spanish shepherd boy named Santiago who travels to Egypt in search of a worldly treasure, discovering self-wisdom along the way.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
description: A novel detailing two days in the life of Holden Caulfield, a disillusioned teenager navigating alienation and identity in New York City.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: The classic fantasy journey of Bilbo Baggins, a home-loving hobbit who is swept into a dangerous quest to raid the treasure hoard of Smaug the dragon.

title: The Handmaid's Tale
author: Margaret Atwood
description: A dystopian vision of a near-future New England under a totalitarian Christian teocracy, exploring themes of female subjugation and agency.

title: The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time
author: Mark Haddon
description: An investigative mystery solved by Christopher John Francis Boone, a mathematically gifted 15-year-old boy on the autism spectrum.

title: A Game of Thrones
author: George R.R. Martin
description: The epic fantasy opener introducing the brutal political maneuvering and civil wars between noble houses for control of the Iron Throne.

title: The Hunger Games
author: Suzanne Collins
description: In a dystopian future nation of Panem, Katniss Everdeen volunteers to take her sister's place in a televised, fight-to-the-death survival tournament.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
description: The seminal gothic horror tale of Victor Frankenstein, a scientist who breathes life into a creature made from scavenged body parts, with catastrophic results.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: The turbulent romantic clash between Elizabeth Bennet, the daughter of a country gentleman, and Fitzwilliam Darcy, a rich aristocratic landowner.

title: The Da Vinci Code
author: Dan Brown
description: A fast-paced thriller following symbologist Robert Langdon as he investigates a murder in the Louvre and uncovers a secret religious society.
